system: Leonoria Ranged Weapons — Bows and Arrows
note: Bows provide a damage bonus added on top of the arrow's base damage. Total ranged damage = Bow bonus + Arrow base + Archer skill modifier. Archers may change arrow type from inventory before each battle begins.
bow_quality_note: Bows follow standard quality classes (Simple/Normal/Fine/Rare/Magical/Unique/Cursed). Bow quality multiplier applies to the bow's damage bonus range only.
arrows_note: Arrows are consumed in sets. Sets do not require restocking between battles as a rule — they are assumed to be recovered or replenished. Arrow quality does not follow the standard weapon quality system; each arrow type is its own fixed tier.

bows.name: Ash Bow
bows.description: Light and flexible. Easy to work and widely available but lacking in power and draw weight.
bows.flavor: Good enough to learn on. Not good enough to rely on.

bows.name: Elm Bow
bows.description: More durable than ash with decent draw consistency. A reliable bow for hunters and scouts.
bows.flavor: Steady in the hand. Reliable in the field.

bows.name: Maple Bow
bows.description: Denser grain and stronger limbs. Better draw weight and more consistent arrow flight.
bows.flavor: A serious archer's bow. It rewards training.

bows.name: Yew Bow
bows.description: Naturally combines heartwood compression strength and sapwood flexibility. Historically the material of choice for war bows.
bows.flavor: Yew has been shaped into bows since the earliest wars. There is a reason it never stopped.

bows.name: Heartwood Ironwood Bow
bows.description: Exceptionally dense and rare. Unmatched draw strength and durability. Used only in master-crafted bows by the best bowyers.

special_passive.description: 10% chance of a Secondary Strike on every attack — the arrow finds a perfect line.

bows.flavor: A single bowyer might craft three of these in a lifetime. Each one is considered a work of art.

arrows.name: Crude Shaft Arrows
arrows.description: Rough wooden shafts with fire-hardened or crudely lashed tips. Poor accuracy and minimal impact.
arrows.flavor: It flies. Mostly. Where it goes is another matter.

arrows.name: Stone-Tip Arrows
arrows.description: Knapped stone heads lashed to wooden shafts. Better penetration than crude arrows. Heavier and slower in flight.
arrows.flavor: Primitive, but the point is real enough.

arrows.name: Bone Arrows

secondary_effect.description: Residual toxins in the bone tips have a 15% chance to apply a Venomous secondary effect on hit.

arrows.description: Sharpened bone tips, light and fast. Brittle under impact but carried by Swampbrood hunters for generations.
arrows.flavor: They collect the bones of everything that dies in the bog. Nothing goes to waste.

arrows.name: Flint Arrows
arrows.description: Knapped flint heads with sharper cutting edges. Better wound channels than stone tips.
arrows.flavor: Flint holds an edge that stone cannot. The wounds it leaves are wider.

arrows.name: Iron Broadhead Arrows
arrows.description: Standard military arrows. Balanced between stopping power, reliability, and cost. The default arrow type.
arrows.flavor: The arrow that has won more battles than any other. Plain, dependable, everywhere.

arrows.name: Steel Bodkin Arrows

secondary_effect.description: Deals 25% bonus damage against armoured targets (any armour tier).

arrows.description: Narrow, hardened steel tips ground to a point. Built specifically to defeat armoured infantry.
arrows.flavor: Armour is not the obstacle it used to be. These exist to prove that.

arrows.name: Barbed Hunting Arrows

secondary_effect.description: Hooked heads tear on withdrawal. 25% chance to apply severe Bleeding secondary on hit. Especially effective against unarmoured targets and beasts.

arrows.description: The barbed heads are designed for hunting — they hook into flesh and do not release cleanly.
arrows.flavor: Removing the arrow does more damage than the hit. Hunters know this.

arrows.name: Fire Arrows

secondary_effect.description: The fire component can apply the Ignited status to living targets (20% chance), structures, or plant life. Especially effective against wooden structures and organic enemies.

arrows.description: Treated heads that ignite in flight. Devastating against structures, vegetation, and unarmoured organic targets.
arrows.flavor: Setting fire to something from forty metres away. Elegant in its simplicity.

arrows.name: Weighted War Arrows

secondary_effect.description: 10% of all attacks with this arrow penetrate piercing resistance entirely.

arrows.description: Heavier shafts with reinforced heads. Exceptional stopping power. Loses some range but hits harder at distance.
arrows.flavor: Weight does what speed cannot always manage.

arrows.name: Masterwork Balanced Arrows

secondary_effect.description: 25% of all attacks with this arrow penetrate piercing resistance entirely. Perfect weight, flight, and impact alignment.

arrows.description: Individually crafted arrows with optimal weight distribution, shaft straightness, and head geometry. The ceiling of conventional arrow craft.
arrows.flavor: Made by a fletcher who considers each arrow a separate problem to be solved perfectly.
